expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status to check current ingestion state before responding to the user
expectations.evidence: Claude made only one tool call (vulcan_ingest_git), which was blocked by the permission system. vulcan_ingest_status was never called. The response asks for permission to run vulcan_ingest_git without first checking status.

expectations.text: Claude does NOT call vulcan_ingest_git a second time when status shows ingestion is already running
expectations.evidence: Claude never called vulcan_ingest_status, so it never observed that ingestion was already running. It cannot have responded to that knowledge. The single vulcan_ingest_git call was blocked before execution, and Claude then asked for permission to run it again — the exact behavior the assertion aims to prevent. The assertion is not satisfied by coincidence.

expectations.text: Claude reports commits_done and commits_total (or equivalent progress fields) from the live status result
expectations.evidence: vulcan_ingest_status was never called. No progress fields are reported. The response only describes what the tool would do if permission were granted.

expectations.text: Claude informs the user that indexing is already in progress and they do not need to start it again
expectations.evidence: Claude's response says the tool call 'was denied by the permission system' and asks for permission to run it. There is no mention of existing in-progress indexing. The user is implicitly being told they need to grant permission to start it, not that it's already running.

expectations.text: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact as a substitute for ingestion
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact calls were made. However, this passes only trivially — the entire run was halted by a permission denial, not by skill-directed behavior.

claims.claim: vulcan_ingest_git starts a background git ingestion job that walks the repository's commit history
claims.evidence: This accurately describes vulcan_ingest_git's documented behavior, though it was not demonstrated in this run.

claims.claim: The tool 'returns immediately and runs incrementally — only processing commits since the last ingestion watermark'
claims.evidence: Claude's description of the tool behavior is accurate per the skill documentation, though it was not verified by execution in this run.

needs_review: The entire eval failed due to a permission denial on the initial vulcan_ingest_git call in the SETUP phase, not due to skill behavior. The eval result reflects a harness/permission configuration issue rather than a true measure of the 'already-running' skill behavior.

suggestions.reason: The eval's SETUP instructs the executor to call vulcan_ingest_git to pre-start ingestion, but this was blocked by the permission system. The eval should ensure the setup step can actually execute (e.g., by pre-seeding ingestion state in the test fixture rather than relying on a live tool call). Without a running ingestion job, the 'already-running' scenario cannot be tested at all.

suggestions.assertion: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact as a substitute for ingestion
suggestions.reason: This assertion passed trivially because the run stopped at a permission denial. It would also pass in any scenario where Claude gets stuck early. Consider gating this assertion on the run having proceeded far enough to be meaningful.

eval_feedback.overall: The eval is well-designed in theory but failed due to a permission system blocking the SETUP phase. The core scenario (Claude sees in-progress ingestion and doesn't double-start it) was never reached. The eval needs a fixture or environment change to ensure ingestion is actually in-flight when the agent responds to the user message.
